topic: OMNIX DevFlow Value-added Optimizations: Hybrid Retrieval, Incremental Linting, Lazy Topological Scanning, and Spotlight Overlays
context: With no more reference repositories left, we finalized the implementation plan and designed four high-value optimizations to elevate the user experience of OMNIX DevFlow.
options_considered: Proceed with the basic feature set mapped directly from reference libraries without further enhancements.

Propose and specify 4 key optimization mechanisms: 1. Hybrid Code Search (LibSQL FTS5 BM25 + Vector RRF); 2. Incremental AI Code Review Linting on Diff; 3. Incremental Tree-sitter hash fingerprinting + Lazy LLM Semantic summaries; 4. Tauri Spotlight clipboard overlay to prevent development context switching.
chosen_option: Implement all 4 value-added optimizations (Hybrid Search, AI Diff review, Lazy summaries, and Spotlight overlay).
reasoning: Search accuracy: Code search requires exact matches for API calls but semantic understanding for logic. Hybrid LibSQL (BM25 + Vector) with RRF is state-of-the-art for this.
Bypassing context switches: Using global keyboard shortcuts and cursor-positioned overlays (Spotlight style) prevents breaking developer focus compared to traditional modal dialogs.

Resource and Cost Efficiency: Big codebases exhaust API tokens and CPU power. Incremental hash fingerprinting with Tree-sitter edge compilation first, followed by lazy semantic summaries, offers a lightning-fast UI response.
Double Guardrails: In-line review of generated diffs by a secondary model detects logic traps that dependencies graph mapping alone might miss.
tradeoffs: Implementing D3.js topologies, Rust bindings, and multiple model routines increases initial UI/Backend complexity: resolved by scheduling modular development milestones.
impact_scope: Tauri main framework (Spotlight overlays, global hotkey registrar)
LibSQL database driver (FTS5 + Vector configurations, RRF merger)
future_risks: Heavy FTS5 index updates during massive git branch checkouts: mitigated by pausing file-system watchers during active git transactions.
